components.name: HEPES
components.evidence: Ca/Mg-HEPES buffer (25 mM HEPES, 2 mM CaCl2, 3 mM MgCl2, pH 7.6)

components.name: calcium chloride
components.evidence: Ca/Mg-HEPES buffer (25 mM HEPES, 2 mM CaCl2, 3 mM MgCl2, pH 7.6)

components.name: magnesium chloride
components.evidence: Ca/Mg-HEPES buffer (25 mM HEPES, 2 mM CaCl2, 3 mM MgCl2, pH 7.6)

components.name: Pseudomonas sp. prey cells
components.role: carbon/nitrogen/energy source (predatory substrate; undefined complex biomass)
components.evidence: Overnight Pseudomonas sp. cultures were standardized ... prey concentration range 10^6-10^9 cells/mL; predator (B. bacteriovorus) preys on the Pseudomonas cells for all its nutrients.

components.name: O2
components.evidence: cultures incubated at 30 degC for 72 h; Bdellovibrio predation performed aerobically.

sources_chased.what: primary paper Materials and Methods (batch predation, section 2.2)
Lambert & Sockett (2008) standard Bdellovibrio culturing (cited for methods, not for a distinct buffer recipe)
sources_chased.how: paper states buffer composition verbatim in main text; no separate recipe reference needed

media.notes: Batch predation medium is a fully-stated defined buffer (25 mM HEPES, 2 mM CaCl2, 3 mM MgCl2, pH 7.6) with NO added glucose, casein, trace elements, vitamins, or reductant; the predator obtains all nutrients from lysed Pseudomonas prey, which is the only complex/undefined ingredient. The chemostat variant (0.05% casein hydrolysate + 25 mg/L glucose) is a different medium and is not part of this batch record.
